text: 6. Arabs plan to adopt stronger anti-Western line
token_text: 6 arabs plan to adopt stronger anti-western line

text:
6. Arabs plan to adopt stronger anti-Western line:

Arab delegates to the United Nations have recommended to their governments that they adopt "a new manner" toward the United States, Britain and France with reference to the Palestine problem. In view of the substantial and continued Western assistance to Israel, these powers should be considered as siding with Tel Aviv and accordingly "their opinion or mediation" should be rejected.

Arab tactics should also include an effort to keep other Arab complaints such as Syria's off the agenda and concentrate on the Israeli attack on the Jordanian village of Kibya.

Comment: Charles Malik's stubborn insistence on 19 October that the UN Security Council address itself to the specific Israeli violation and not to the larger issues seems to reflect this recommendation. It probably foreshadows a bitter and intemperate Arab position in the forthcoming sessions
